headline: Agent tooling techniques led the quarter while vector search and HNSW broke out around retrieval infrastructure.
body: The quarter's rotation favored agent construction techniques, with Agent SDK, tool context, task decomposition, and AI-assisted development all moving together as production agent workflows matured. Vector search and HNSW broke out alongside new entrants in failure analysis, retry logic, and session isolation, reflecting attention on retrieval and robustness as agents run longer. Memory remained a central constraint: Baseten presented key-value cache compression for long-horizon agents, and Ben Geist argued efficiency comes from better context and entropy reduction rather than scaling compute [2][3]. Documentary research cited 80% of organizations deploying agents but only 10% feeling in control, framing the human oversight gap [6].

cited_items.title: AI Agents: How Do We Take Back Control? | Trailer
